config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena kalau tidak, kekuasaan mudah disalahgunakan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan menginjak kebebasan dasar seseorang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dilindungi dengan kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should generally take priority over individual rights, because in the United States we have seen how quickly “the common good” can become an excuse for overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since laws against violence, fraud, and public harm are necessary for everyone to live together safely. The real test is whether a limit on personal freedom is narrowly justified and accountable, not whether it simply sounds beneficial to the majority. If society always wins by default, then minority rights and dissent become fragile, and that is a dangerous tradeoff.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia saya melihat “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam suara yang lemah atau minoritas. Memang benar hak individu tidak absolut, seperti soal kekerasan, penipuan, atau bahaya publik, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau prioritas selalu diberikan ke masyarakat secara umum, tanpa perlindungan kuat bagi individu, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa atas nama kebaikan bersama. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu menang atas individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi kebebasan pribadi sambil tetap menjaga ketertiban bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, history keeps showing that once “the public good” becomes the default trump card, it can be used to silence dissent, crush minorities, or expand government power beyond what is justified. Individual rights do have limits, but those limits should be narrow, clearly defined, and subject to real accountability, not handed over whenever a majority claims it is for everyone’s benefit. A healthy society protects people first, because rights are what keep society from turning into simple majority rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia saya melihat alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan individu yang lemah, berbeda, atau kritis. Memang ada keadaan tertentu di mana hak individu perlu dibatasi, tetapi pembatasan itu harus ketat, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena dianggap lebih menguntungkan mayoritas. Kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kuasa dengan dalih kebaikan bersama. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang melindungi hak pribadi terlebih dahulu, lalu membangun kepentingan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am wary of any argument that lets the majority or the state override personal liberty just because it is framed as the greater good, since that has historically been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and overreach. At the same time, I do not think rights mean anarchy; limited restrictions are justified when they are narrowly tied to real harms like violence, fraud, or serious public danger. To me, the burden should always be on society to prove why a right must be limited, not on the individual to prove why freedom deserves protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
